headline: Broadcom CEO Sees AI Chip Sales Topping $100 Billion in 2027
summary: The company projects that AI chip revenue will be $10.7 billion in the current quarter, so reaching an annual pace of $100 billion would be a major jump.  Broadcom reported $20 billion in AI sales in 2025.

content.title: Broadcom CEO Sees AI Chip Sales Topping $100 Billion in 2027
content.summary: The company projects that AI chip revenue will be $10.7 billion in the current quarter, so reaching an annual pace of $100 billion would be a major jump.  Broadcom reported $20 billion in AI sales in 2025.

headline: Dassault Systèmes Deepens AI Role In Drug Development And Manufacturing
summary: Medidata, a Dassault Systèmes (ENXTPA:DSY) brand, received top innovation ratings for its AI driven clinical trial solutions and a Luminary designation for its role in FDA novel drug approvals. Medidata’s AI platforms are used across a large share of FDA novel drug approvals, supporting trial efficiencies and digital workflows in clinical research. Dassault Systèmes expanded its healthcare footprint through a new collaboration between Medidata and CRIO focused on clinical data...

headline: Nokia’s AI MWC26 Push Tests Long Term Growth And Margin Story
summary: Nokia Oyj (HLSE:NOKIA) used Mobile World Congress 2026 to announce a series of AI-led collaborations and product launches. The company unveiled new AI-RAN, open network automation, and agentic AI network services with partners including Telia Finland, Google Cloud, Deutsche Telekom, TIM Brasil, and Nvidia. Nokia also introduced next generation, AI ready radio products with a focus on energy efficiency and sustainable network build outs. Nokia enters this AI push with its shares at €7.032...

headline: What Intel (INTC)'s Board Transition and AI Partnerships Shift Means For Shareholders
summary: Recently, Intel announced that longtime board chair Frank Yeary will retire after the May 13, 2026 shareholder meeting, with semiconductor veteran Dr. Craig H. Barratt becoming independent chair, while Infosys and Corvex revealed new AI collaborations leveraging Intel platforms and Intel Trust Domain Extensions to secure and scale enterprise and GPU-accelerated workloads. These moves highlight Intel’s push to embed its CPUs, Gaudi accelerators, foundry security features, and 18A roadmap more...
